128059.explanation: Although a mix of different people is always nice, for us the abilities of a person come first.

128060.explanation: We consider a referendum an important part of democracy. As far as we are concerned, it should be used when necessary.

128063.explanation: Sufficient space must be provided to be able to continue entrepreneurship. Practical cooperation with our entrepreneurial residents, without unnecessary (expensive) bureaucracy. Providing clarity and making clear agreements is most important.

128064.explanation: We see an important role for Stichting WindCent to help make these initiatives possible.

128065.explanation: ONPL has no firm opinion on this; in our view, it should be assessed per application whether establishment is appropriate or not.

128067.explanation: Housing seasonal workers at agricultural companies must continue to be encouraged, provided it fits the area and good agreements are made. It is important that this is encouragement rather than imposition, and ONPL does not exclude other initiatives.

128068.explanation: This is currently policy in the municipality and ONPL does not intend to change that. However, we continue to call for native trees to be replanted in particular.

128073.explanation: ONPL stands for less bureaucracy and relaxation of rules. Tightening the noise standard does not fit with this.

128075.explanation: This conflicts with rules from the province. ONPL supports customization but also finds it important to preserve our valuable countryside.

128076.explanation: Being a member of a sports club is much more than just playing sports. It is not only good for physical health but also for mental health. In addition, it gives a feeling of belonging and mattering. This must be possible for everyone.

128080.explanation: ONPL finds self-reliance important for sports clubs and therefore does not see a primary role for the municipality here. However, we also value a diverse range of sports and want to work to preserve that.

128083.explanation: Safety is very important. Our municipality has a large rural area with many businesses and unfortunately many burglaries or attempted burglaries. More police presence to prevent this is seen as an added value.

128084.explanation: ONPL considers camera surveillance in public places permissible, especially if it promotes the safety and sense of security of our residents.

128089.explanation: As far as ONPL is concerned, a holiday home is really a holiday home. We want to prevent uncontrolled growth of permanent residence.

128091.explanation: ONPL agrees with this, provided these people are physically and mentally able.

128092.explanation: We want to provide ongoing support to people who cannot afford this.

128093.explanation: The municipality recently changed the income limit from 120% to 130% of the social minimum. This means even more residents qualify for the 'Participation Scheme'. Therefore, ONPL currently finds it unnecessary to raise this again.

128095.explanation: We find this depends on the situation. Attention should mainly be given where demand is high. Places should not be created unnecessarily.

128097.explanation: ONPL is always in favor of freedom of choice. People should always have the option here, although sometimes that means it may not be available in a small municipality like Zundert.

128098.explanation: ONPL mainly values the price-quality ratio and prefers to cut back in this area only where it offers no added value; otherwise, it does not.

128100.explanation: ONPL is satisfied with the current tourist tax.

128101.explanation: ONPL is not necessarily opposed to new industrial estates, but we want to first fill and possibly expand the current industrial sites before creating new ones.

128104.explanation: Safe swimming is crucial for every child. Municipal funding should be possible where people cannot afford it themselves.

128107.explanation: We see this happening in large (student) cities, but find it inappropriate for Zundert due to its village character.

128108.explanation: ONPL finds an adequate parking supply important, and we would like to see supply and demand aligned.

128109.explanation: Although we appreciate a low-car city center, we find a car-free zone a step too far. It is important that our entrepreneurs remain easily accessible and that people with mobility impairments can park nearby.

128111.explanation: ONPL finds it important to address the traffic issues especially at the Risten. Whether the bypass road is the best solution will have to become clear in the future. Traffic safety is paramount.

128113.explanation: Research has shown this is the only suitable location for building a Child Center. Due to choices made in previous council terms, other suitable sites have meanwhile been assigned different uses. ONPL therefore sees no other option than the Hoge Dreef location.

128059.explanation: CDA-Zundert wants the right people in the right positions. The new mayor and aldermen’s board should be formed based on qualities.

128060.explanation: Simplifying complex issues to a simple "yes" or "no" choice can increase societal divisions. We support citizen participation and input, close by, focused on the interests of our residents.

128064.explanation: CDA-Zundert encourages local initiatives for sustainable energy. Any financial support does not necessarily have to come from the municipality.

128065.explanation: CDA-Zundert encourages development of existing work locations for local logistics and innovation. We also endorse the national plea against 'urbanization sprawl.'

128067.explanation: CDA-Zundert considers small-scale housing for seasonal workers most appropriate. If possible for the entrepreneur on their own premises, this is desirable.

128068.explanation: CDA-Zundert chooses greening and biodiversity, but not through rigid rules. We look at the quality and effect of greenery, not just the numbers.

128073.explanation: Under the zoning plan, the municipality can set specific noise standards. CDA-Zundert believes the regulatory framework should fit the location and nature of the festival or event. Permit conditions can be tightened if the nuisance is consistently perceived as high.

128075.explanation: It is pleasant to live in our municipality of Zundert, including the rural areas. Expansion in rural zones should be possible as long as the agricultural landscape is preserved.

128076.explanation: Everyone can participate—that is important to CDA-Zundert. We want to keep the path to participation schemes as simple as possible.

128080.explanation: Where necessary, the municipality must provide structural support. This applies to human resources and accommodations.

128083.explanation: CDA-Zundert supports more visible and strengthened deployment of enforcement officers.

128084.explanation: For CDA-Zundert, more camera surveillance is not a solution. We choose safety through connection: a recognizable enforcement officer (boa) and residents watching out for each other. Privacy and human contact come first. People should feel safe because they know each other, not because they are being filmed.

128089.explanation: Zundert focuses on regular housing construction in all communities. Permanent residence in recreational homes does not fit with careful use of rural areas and puts pressure on livability and facilities.

128091.explanation: Participation is important, but CDA-Zundert chooses activation based on personal strength and talents. Voluntary participation and appropriate guidance work better than mandatory counter-performance.

128092.explanation: Stopping exemption from payment undermines existential security and therefore poses a risk to social stability in our municipality.

128093.explanation: CDA-Zundert believes help with poverty should be effective, humane, and targeted. Our current system offers a solid foundation that reaches the most vulnerable residents.

128095.explanation: We stimulate sustainability but opt for customization per community. Additional charging stations should match local needs and livability, without rigid obligations.

128097.explanation: Personal values are important. Freedom of choice comes first, as long as care remains accessible, affordable, and locally executable.

128098.explanation: The focus should be on the quality of care and simpler access. Strong local teams can achieve this.

128100.explanation: CDA-Zundert sees the tourist tax as a fair contribution from visitors to local facilities and the maintenance of our beautiful rural surroundings. This keeps the burden lower for our own residents while allowing us to continue investing in an attractive and livable municipality for everyone.

128101.explanation: CDA-Zundert foresees sufficient response to future-proof businesses through modernization of existing sites.

128104.explanation: There are easily accessible facilities for children in families where swimming lessons cannot be paid for.

128107.explanation: CDA-Zundert is against a ban. However, stronger enforcement fits this position. Strict enforcement must be possible on rental properties causing nuisance because this affects the livability of our village(s).

128108.explanation: Our current parking standard matches the parking demand in our municipality.

128109.explanation: CDA-Zundert supports “safe traffic.” Where necessary and possible, 30 km/h zones. And improving school bike routes.

128111.explanation: For the Rijsbergen bypass, we take a next step. Choices are made together, based on existing management plans and feasible finances.

128113.explanation: CDA-Zundert is steadfast. Realization of a new Child Center (education and childcare) in 2029, including appropriate sports facilities.

128059.explanation: For the Zundert VVD, quality comes first. It is about having the best people for Zundert in the college. Whether men, women, or an equal distribution does not determine this for us. Cooperation and strengthening do.

128060.explanation: We prefer good participation, polls, and prior consultation with clear decision-making afterward. For the Zundert VVD, the moment to make choices is at the elections. Referenda add little: issues are often too complex for a yes/no question.

128063.explanation: The VVD of Zundert is not in favor of automatically allowing secondary activities on every farm. However, the municipality should facilitate farmers under clear conditions for appropriate secondary activities, such as a shop or campsite, that fit the business and the environment.

128064.explanation: The VVD of Zundert believes that the municipality should primarily facilitate and encourage. There are national and regional subsidies for sustainable energy. Initiatives are most successful when residents take the lead themselves, with a municipality that thinks along and supports, rather than intervening financially.

128065.explanation: Zundert is an agricultural municipality with many small and medium-sized enterprises and associated employment. Large-scale logistic distribution centers are mainly suitable for regions with direct motorway connections. We do not want an increase in truck traffic in the municipality of Zundert without infrastructural solutions.

128067.explanation: Seasonal workers are indispensable for Zundert and deserve good housing. Where possible, housing on company premises is logical and desirable, but entrepreneurs should not be compelled. Many entrepreneurs feel responsible themselves for decent housing, which is also in their own interest.

128068.explanation: The VVD of Zundert does not want to burden residents with extra rules. Felling and replanting trees is a personal responsibility. The municipality should replant with its own projects and involve entrepreneurs in greening where possible, without imposing new obligations.

128073.explanation: Clear noise regulations already apply to festivals and parties. The VVD of Zundert finds it important that events do not cause nuisance but does not want to impose additional restrictions. Events are part of village life; extra regulatory pressure is unnecessary.

128075.explanation: The rural area is intended for agricultural use and forms the green working area of Zundert. The VVD of Zundert opposes building houses on farmland without due cause. Only with careful zoning plan amendments can housing be considered.

128076.explanation: The VVD of Zundert believes that municipal schemes should be the same for everyone: either for all residents or for none. Suitable schemes already exist for children from low-income families, such as the Youth, Sports and Culture Fund.

128080.explanation: The VVD of Zundert wants to help associations with challenges beyond their control or capacity. Subsidies and schemes exist for this. At the same time, associations should know in advance what to expect from the municipality. It is not the municipality's task to maintain associations in case of structural deficits.

128083.explanation: More visible enforcement officers contribute to safety and a secure feeling in the neighborhood. The VVD of Zundert wants to deploy local enforcement officers especially for enforcement and tackling antisocial behavior; nuisance must be addressed promptly and decisively.

128084.explanation: In case of recurring nuisance, camera surveillance can help. The VVD of Zundert opts for targeted and temporary surveillance, for example with mobile cameras. This works preventively against nuisance and drug trafficking, respecting privacy and with clear agreements.

128089.explanation: Recreational homes are, in principle, not intended for permanent residence. The VVD of Zundert does not choose general permission but wants tailor-made solutions where necessary. Additionally, we strive for structural solutions.

128091.explanation: The VVD of Zundert finds it important that people contribute to society according to their ability. Those who receive social assistance benefits and are physically and mentally capable can do socially meaningful work as a step towards reintegration and paid employment.

128092.explanation: Resources are scarce; equal treatment for all. Everyone must pay taxes; hardworking residents should not bear the burdens of those who cannot. Tailored solutions can be considered.

128093.explanation: The VVD of Zundert believes that work should always pay off. Therefore, we oppose automatically raising income thresholds. However, thresholds should grow where necessary with the cost of living. We choose indexing and tailor-made solutions, with attention to individual situations.

128095.explanation: Parking spaces in Zundert are scarce. The VVD of Zundert believes this space should remain available to everyone. We opt for smart sharing and appropriate solutions, instead of increasingly reserving parking spaces exclusively for one type of car.

128097.explanation: Appropriate care is an important goal but not always possible. The pressure on care is great, especially in emergencies. Care must then be available immediately. When there is time and space, someone should be able to choose a care provider that suits them.

128098.explanation: In youth care, quality must come first. The VVD of Zundert focuses on effectiveness: the right help at the right time, with a good balance between quality, supply, and price. Selecting based on price alone shortchanges children.

128100.explanation: Tourists enjoy all the beauty Zundert has to offer and put pressure on facilities and infrastructure. It is reasonable that they contribute via tourist tax. We also keep an eye on other regions to maintain proportionality and avoid driving tourists away.

128101.explanation: Businesses provide employment and a strong local economy. The VVD of Zundert wants to offer space for entrepreneurship. With suitable business parks, we can retain and help businesses grow.

128104.explanation: School swimming is part of education and is primarily the responsibility of schools. The municipality provides good swimming facilities. Schools can organize and facilitate school swimming themselves, fitting their educational program.

128107.explanation: A ban on renting out rooms individually means homes can only be rented out as a whole. This reduces supply and increases pressure on the housing market. We opt for more living space, with clear rules to prevent nuisance.

128108.explanation: In many neighborhoods, parking pressure is high, especially on weekends. This leads to illegal parking and nuisance. The VVD of Zundert believes there should be enough parking spaces without making housing more expensive or unnecessarily hindering housing plans.

128109.explanation: The VVD of Zundert opts for an accessible center. Visitors must be able to park close to shops and facilities. Parking space is limited; losing parking spaces in Molenstraat makes the center less accessible. The center must also remain accessible for its residents.

128111.explanation: Traffic pressure in Rijsbergen has been high for years. Studies show that a bypass will fundamentally improve livability and traffic safety in the core. This makes a permanent solution necessary, alongside temporary measures, provided it is carefully integrated.

128113.explanation: The VVD of Zundert is in favor of a good and future-proof child center but opposes building on a site that requires expropriation when alternatives exist. Expropriation should only be used as a last resort. Other locations must be seriously explored first.

128059.explanation: D66 Zundert strives for balance. Quality takes precedence over gender.

128060.explanation: D66 Zundert supports the advisory referendum.

128063.explanation: D66 Zundert considers desirable those matters that benefit tourist Zundert, as long as they align with business operations.

128064.explanation: D66 Zundert believes that this transition should be subsidized.

128065.explanation: D66 believes that this depends on the location and the thematic character of the distribution center.

128067.explanation: D66 Zundert supports this but prefers qualitative, small-scale housing.

128068.explanation: For D66 Zundert, this is a must and we like to set requirements for trunk thickness, location, and crown.

128073.explanation: D66 Zundert considers limiting health risks desirable and therefore enforcement of these requirements is necessary.

128075.explanation: D66 Zundert wants to stimulate entrepreneurship and preservation of the countryside. Building should be considered per situation.

128076.explanation: D66 Zundert believes that a municipal contribution should continue to exist.

128080.explanation: Based on a sport vision to be developed, D66 Zundert believes there should be as diverse a sports offer as possible.

128083.explanation: In general, D66 Zundert finds that more can be done on safety. Enforcement officers are a crucial part of this.

128084.explanation: D66 Zundert values both privacy and safety. Permanent camera surveillance has no role here.

128089.explanation: D66 Zundert believes that a zoning plan amendment should underlie this.

128091.explanation: D66 Zundert wants to look at possibilities instead of impossibilities.

128092.explanation: D66 Zundert believes that people below or at the social assistance threshold should have the option to apply for it.

128093.explanation: D66 Zundert sees increasing poverty that can be combated by raising this threshold.

128095.explanation: D66 Zundert wants the municipality to continue and encourage the current policy.

128097.explanation: D66 Zundert believes this is not always possible in the municipality of Zundert. The quality of care takes precedence over faith, culture, or belief. However, D66 thinks the municipality should be able to facilitate this regionally.

128098.explanation: D66 Zundert values both quality and price. We believe youth care can be more efficient.

128100.explanation: D66 Zundert finds that this is currently low for our municipality.

128101.explanation: D66 Zundert believes existing business parks must be fully occupied before considering expansion.

128104.explanation: D66 Zundert considers swimming lessons very important but does not want to increase pressure on education.

128107.explanation: D66 Zundert sees opportunities in diversity of housing types to solve the housing shortage. For new construction, we want a self-occupancy obligation.

128108.explanation: D66 Zundert prefers to invest in promoting cycling and walking.

128109.explanation: D66 Zundert believes that a car-free center contributes to safety and coziness.

128111.explanation: Although D66 Zundert is basically against extra asphalt and for preserving nature, we see no other choice than a bypass around Rijsbergen. This relieves traffic in the village center.

128113.explanation: D66 Zundert believes the Hoge Dreef is the only suitable location for the community center with space for growth, green education, and other important matters such as sports and culture.

128059.explanation: We look at the qualities, character, and suitability of applicants for this demanding role and do not discriminate based on characteristics such as gender, origin, religion, or ethnicity.

128060.explanation: An advisory referendum can give the College insight into what residents feel, but the question formulation greatly affects the outcome. Sometimes problems are too complex for a referendum, and besides, why hold these elections then?

128063.explanation: This must be considered case by case and we can be lenient. A shop or side activity is no problem, but a campsite goes too far given our past issues. Always allowing this is too much for LP. Less strict: Yes! Full freedom: No! Always allowing or granting permissions makes no sense in a dossier like Spatial Planning, where the art is to weigh options.

128064.explanation: Network congestion is already a problem and financial support to residents will not solve it. Residents are better off investing money in insulation and energy storage. Generation requires national and provincial coordination.

128065.explanation: These centers offer relatively little employment to residents and the revenues often go to foreign companies. Distribution centers for Dutch needs already exist, and further industrialization of the landscape is undesirable.

128067.explanation: Agreed, housing up to a maximum of 50 people on private property is preferred. LP is not in favor of large-scale shelters. Employers can also choose alternative small-scale (up to 50 people) housing if private property is not possible.

128068.explanation: We do not want to impose this on every tree, but some trees deserve this status for replanting. Additionally, other efforts can be made to add more trees in the municipality that do not necessarily have to be planted in the same place. Always replanting is a strict requirement. Many trees have been added already without a registration obligation.

128073.explanation: We leave enforcement to the national government and the police.

128075.explanation: Given regulations such as 50m drift zones and mutual nuisance between farmers and residents in rural areas, we would rather convert farms/companies with favorable arrangements into residential neighborhoods in core-edge areas. LP supports deregulation and active land policy to create more housing.

128076.explanation: Lokaal Perspectief is for a safety net, not a hammock. The difference between working and not working is currently too small. The large number of different funds for combating poverty costs a lot to implement and needs to be simplified.

128080.explanation: The municipality will not have money for that in the coming years; financially, the municipality is already on the edge. Most associations already receive some form of subsidy.

128083.explanation: This is the task of the police, not the municipality.

128084.explanation: There are already thousands of video doorbells and cameras known to the neighborhood officers. Privacy is very important; more camera surveillance adds no value.

128089.explanation: Given the housing shortage and various regulations that hinder permits and plans, this can be used as an occasional emergency measure to ease the pressure. This should not be used on a large scale but is a matter of customization. Otherwise, we risk problems like Fort Oranje again.

128091.explanation: Work ennobles. Lokaal Perspectief is for a safety net, not a hammock.

128092.explanation: The Tax Cooperation and municipality already forgive almost nothing, so this does not matter much.

128093.explanation: The municipality itself suffers from poverty; we as residents must arrange more among ourselves and rely less on governments. We refer you back to The Hague. We will not hinder you from helping each other.

128095.explanation: Municipality of Zundert is not the Municipality of Amsterdam. Charging at home is cheapest and should be encouraged. Use the car as a mobile home battery.

128097.explanation: Care organizations already arrange for which clients and caregivers come into contact. Sometimes there is a click, sometimes not, and then another caregiver comes next time. This is not a municipal task.

128098.explanation: Unfortunately, that is the financial situation we find ourselves in as a municipality.

128100.explanation: There is not much tourism here, but consideration can be given to having labor migrants contribute. The rate does not need to increase, but the target group can be expanded.

128101.explanation: Especially for SMEs and to relocate existing businesses in village centers with a "large environmental category radius" favorably to allow developments.

128107.explanation: Better a room than no room. LP believes the municipality should not introduce additional rules that further disrupt the housing market, in addition to disastrous measures already taken by The Hague.

128108.explanation: In Diepstraat at V.V. Wernhout, yes. Creating more parking spaces also means more expensive housing... or less green space. It very much depends on the situation; it is mainly important to carefully consider this in new construction.

128109.explanation: This is only an option when the traffic relief (bypass) is also ready for Zundert, this naturally also applies to Rijsbergen.

128111.explanation: Through traffic and heavy agricultural vehicles must be excluded from the center yet still have a route around it. A ring road also provides an opportunity to develop a vision for the center of Rijsbergen, thereby increasing livability.

128113.explanation: KC Hoge Dreef is becoming too expensive over time due to possible procedures and damage compensations, and realization will take years. LP prefers a primary school on municipally owned land.

128059.explanation: This is a good aspiration, not a strict requirement. For OPD, the capacities and motivation of individuals are decisive.

128060.explanation: It is important that citizens' input is used in time. There are several ways to do this. Conversations often yield valuable information that is missing in a referendum. For complex issues, for example, a citizens' assembly works better than a referendum.

128063.explanation: OPD wants farmers to have the opportunity to diversify their businesses with small-scale, appropriate activities. What is appropriate must be discussed in consultation with the immediate environment.

128064.explanation: OPD wants to stimulate cooperation. We see opportunities to support joint projects by residents via Windcent (wind turbines A16). Regarding making homes more sustainable, OPD wants to inform people well through the Zundert Energy Desk.

128065.explanation: OPD wants to provide space for local entrepreneurs. Warehouses must fit within the landscape. The establishment of large distribution centers in our rural area is not desired. At BCT, much space has been allocated to tree cultivation-related activities.

128067.explanation: OPD finds it important that housing for employees at agricultural businesses can continue. The accommodation must be of good quality and healthy.

128068.explanation: Trees provide cooling and purify the air. OPD wants to be careful with existing trees. When trees need to be cut down, replacement is necessary. This applies to both young and old trees. We want to better regulate this in the felling policy.

128073.explanation: There are noise standards at festivals and parties. However, the noise can sometimes be heard over a large distance. Hearing damage is a common problem. OPD would like advice from the Public Health Service (GGD) on the noise standard. It is important that compliance is checked randomly or upon complaints.

128075.explanation: It cannot be the case that houses are built on any random piece of land.

128076.explanation: Sports are very important. It is healthy, educational, and you meet many people. OPD wants to prevent children from not participating in sports because they cannot afford it.

128080.explanation: A club is responsible for its own finances. It is up to the club to organize activities with volunteers, sponsors, and membership fees.

128083.explanation: The number of enforcement officers (boa's) has increased in recent years. Young people have joined. It is important to maintain this number and ensure these people continue working here long term. We must retain knowledge and experience to work purposefully.

128084.explanation: According to OPD, speed enforcement with cameras can be expanded. Speeding occurs too often. We are cautious about placing cameras to monitor people's behavior; this should only happen in exceptional cases.

128089.explanation: A holiday home must remain a holiday home.

128091.explanation: Volunteering gives meaning and can help find paid work. Unfortunately, not everyone with social assistance benefits can volunteer. It therefore cannot be a requirement.

128092.explanation: Unfortunately, there are families with insufficient income for daily groceries. We want to continue supporting people who need help.

128093.explanation: We have only recently raised the threshold. It is important that people know they can get help. The Zundert Information Point provides information and advice on questions.

128095.explanation: The number of electric cars is growing. Work is already underway to expand the number of public charging points. Agreements have been made about this. Depending on the neighborhood, the municipality may have to further expand the number of public charging points over time.

128097.explanation: As a municipality, we will not restrict freedom of choice. However, the supply depends on the availability of suitable entrepreneurs/organizations active in the region.

128098.explanation: Quality and availability are important. If a cheap provider has very long waiting times, things are not going well. Price is therefore not the most important criterion to consider.

128100.explanation: OPD is not opposed to a limited increase in tourist tax. We must remain attractive to tourists.

128101.explanation: We do not want an increase in number. However, we keep the option open to enlarge existing business parks near the centers.

128104.explanation: For safety, it is important that every child learns to swim. We contribute financially to the Wildert swimming pool.

128107.explanation: It must be possible to rent out a room separately under conditions. If the house is too large to use entirely by oneself, renting a room can help someone else find housing.

128108.explanation: In most neighborhoods, the current parking standards are sufficient. We would like to improve public transport and become less dependent on cars.

128109.explanation: OPD wants fewer cars to drive through the center of Zundert, but a complete ban on cars goes too far. Shops and homes must remain accessible.

128111.explanation: There is too much through traffic in the center of Rijsbergen and the Risten. With population growth, this will increase. A bypass (partial ring road) is necessary. Now work on feasibility and route. Include safety for Bredaseweg and Antwerpseweg in this. Begin introducing a 30 km/h zone in the village.

128113.explanation: OPD wants a new school quickly. It is uncertain whether the land at Hoge Dreef can be purchased. OPD would have found it more logical to choose location(s) owned by the municipality. Much time and money have already been spent on Hoge Dreef. We hope for a swift agreement with the owners on purchase.

statements.title: Rijsbergen should get a bypass road.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The center of Zundert should become car-free.
Zundert should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on public safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The residents of Zundert should be able to give their opinion on important issues through a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Zundert moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: Zundert should have more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the neighborhoods.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: Zundert should have more business/industrial parks.
A child center should be built on the Hoge Dreef in Zundert.
